%% DSU structure implementation adapted for Erlang
%% We use maps for sparse representation if N were huge, but since N <= 5*10^4, 
%% arrays (lists) are fine for performance if managed correctly.

%% Internal DSU state structure:
%% {parent: list(integer), parity: list(integer)}
%% parity[i] = XOR sum of weights on path i -> parent[i]

dsu_new(N) ->
    %% Initialize parent[i] = i, parity[i] = 0 for all i in 0..N-1
    Parents = lists:seq(0, N-1, 1),
    Parities = lists:replicate(N, 0),
    {Parents, Parities}.

dsu_find(DSU, I) ->
    case DSU of
        {Parents, Parities} ->
            case lists:nth(I, Parents) of
                Root when Root == I ->
                    %% Root found
                    {I, 0};
                Parent ->
                    %% Recurse up
                    {Root, ParityRoot} = dsu_find(DSU, Parent),
                    
                    %% Path compression and parity update
                    OldParityI = lists:nth(I, Parities),
                    NewParityI = OldParityI `xor` ParityRoot,
                    
                    NewParents = lists:replace(Parents, I, Root),
                    NewParities = lists:replace(Parities, I, NewParityI),
                    
                    {Root, NewParityI}
            end
        _ -> error(dsu_invalid_state)
    end.

dsu_union(DSU, U, V, W) ->
    case DSU of
        {Parents, Parities} ->
            {RootU, ParityU} = dsu_find(DSU, U),
            {RootV, ParityV} = dsu_find(DSU, V),
            
            if RootU == RootV ->
                %% Cycle check: Cycle Parity = P(u->rootU) XOR W XOR P(v->rootV)
                CycleParity = ParityU `xor` W `xor` ParityV,
                
                if CycleParity == 0 ->
                    %% Even cycle, no state change needed for DSU structure itself, 
                    %% but this function is typically used for merging. 
                    %% Since we only call union when roots differ, this case should not happen 
                    %% for the main logic, but handling it defensively.
                    {DSU, false} 
                else
                    %% Odd cycle, rejected implicitly by main loop logic.
                    {DSU, false}
                end;
            else
                %% Merge V's set into U's set (RootU becomes new parent of RootV)
                
                %% Required parity for RootV -> RootU:
                %% Path: RootV --(ParityV)--> V --(W)--> U --(ParityU)--> RootU
                %% Required Parity(RootV -> RootU) = ParityV XOR W XOR ParityU
                NewParityRootV = ParityV `xor` W `xor` ParityU,
                
                NewParents = lists:replace(Parents, RootV, RootU),
                NewParities = lists:replace(Parities, RootV, NewParityRootV),
                
                NewDSU = {NewParents, NewParities},
                {NewDSU, true}
            end
        _ -> error(dsu_invalid_state)
    end.

xor(A, B) when is_integer(A), is_integer(B) ->
    % Assuming inputs are 0 or 1, XOR is equivalent to addition modulo 2.
    (A + B) rem 2;

        {[], _} -> error(index_out_of_bounds)
        {[_Head], Tail} ->

                {[_Elem], _} -> Elem
                _ -> error(index_out_of_bounds)
            end
        _ -> error(internal_error)
    end.

lists:seq(Start, End, Step) ->
    if Start > End -> []
    else [Start | lists:seq(Start + Step, End, Step)]
    end.

lists:replicate(N, Element) ->
    lists:seq(0, N-1, 1) >>= fun(_I) -> [Element] end.

lists:split(N, K, L) ->
    case lists:split(N, K) of
        {Head, Tail} when length(Head) == K -> {Head, Tail};
        _ -> error(internal_split_error)
    end.

    InitialDSU = dsu_new(N),
    AddedEdgesCount = 0,

    process_edges(InitialDSU, Edges, 0, AddedEdgesCount).

process_edges(DSU, [], _Count, FinalCount) ->
    FinalCount;

process_edges(DSU, [[U, V, W] | RestEdges], CurrentCount, FinalCount) ->
    
    {RootU, ParityU} = dsu_find(DSU, U),
    {RootV, ParityV} = dsu_find(DSU, V),
    
    if RootU == RootV ->
        %% Cycle check
        CycleParity = ParityU `xor` W `xor` ParityV,
        
        if CycleParity == 0 ->
            %% Even cycle, add edge
            NewCount = CurrentCount + 1,
            process_edges(DSU, RestEdges, CurrentCount, NewCount)
        else
            %% Odd cycle, reject edge
            NewCount = CurrentCount,
            process_edges(DSU, RestEdges, CurrentCount, NewCount)
        end;
    else
        %% Different components, safe to add
        
        %% Perform union. Since roots are different, union always succeeds structurally.
        {NewDSU, _Success} = dsu_union(DSU, U, V, W),
        
        NewCount = CurrentCount + 1,
        process_edges(NewDSU, RestEdges, CurrentCount, NewCount)
    end.

    Parent = list_to_map(lists:zip(lists:seq(0, N-1), lists:seq(0, N-1))),
    Parity = list_to_map(lists:zip(lists:seq(0, N-1), lists:seq(0, N-1))),

dsu_find(State, I) ->
    case maps:find(I, State#parent) of
        {ok, P} when P == I ->
            % Root found
            {State, I, 0}; % {newState, root, parity_to_root}
        {ok, P} ->
            % Not root, recurse
            case dsu_find(State, P) of
                {State', Root, Parity_P_to_Root} ->
                    % Path compression and parity update
                    % New parity[I] = old_parity[I] XOR Parity(P -> Root)
                    Old_Parity_I_to_P = maps:get(I, State#parity, 0),
                    New_Parity_I_to_Root = Old_Parity_I_to_P `xor` Parity_P_to_Root,
                    
                    New_Parent = maps:put(I, Root, State#parent),
                    New_Parity = maps:put(I, New_Parity_I_to_Root, State#parity),
                    State'#{parent => New_Parent, parity => New_Parity},
                    {State'#{parent => New_Parent, parity => New_Parity}, Root, New_Parity_I_to_Root}
        _ ->
            % Should not happen if initialization is correct
            error(dsu_find_error)
    end.

dsu_union(State, U, V, W) ->
    % Find roots and path parities
    {State1, RootU, ParityU_to_RootU} = dsu_find(State, U),
    {State2, RootV, ParityV_to_RootV} = dsu_find(State1, V),
    
    if RootU == RootV ->
        {State2, false} % Already connected, union not performed
    else
        % Merge V's set into U's set (RootU becomes parent of RootV)
        
        % We need to set parity[RootV] such that:
        % Parity(RootV -> RootU) = Parity(RootV -> V) XOR W XOR Parity(U -> RootU)
        % P(RootV -> V) = ParityV_to_RootV (since V is path-compressed to RootV)
        % P(U -> RootU) = ParityU_to_RootU

        % P(RootV -> RootU) = ParityV_to_RootV `xor` W `xor` ParityU_to_RootU

        New_Parity = maps:put(RootV, New_Parity_RootV_to_RootU, State2#parity),

        fun(State, Edge) ->

            {StateU, RootU, ParityU_to_RootU} = dsu_find(State, U),
            {StateV, RootV, ParityV_to_RootV} = dsu_find(StateU, V),

                if CycleParity == 0 ->

                else

                    {StateV, 0}

                {StateMerged, 1}
            end

        % Here, ParityU is P(u -> rootU), ParityV is P(v -> rootV).
        % We set parent[rootV] = rootU. We need parity[rootV] = P(rootV -> rootU).
        % Path: rootV --(ParityV)--> v --(w)--> u --(ParityU)--> rootU
        % Parity(rootV -> rootU) = ParityV XOR w XOR ParityU
        
        NewParityRootV = ParityV `xor` w `xor` ParityU,
        
        NewParent = lists:replace(Dsu#dsu.parent, RootV, RootU),
        NewParityList = lists:replace(Dsu#dsu.parity, RootV, NewParityRootV),
        NewDsu = Dsu#{parent => NewParent, parity => NewParityList},
        
        {NewDsu, true}
    end.

%% Helper for XOR operation on integers
xor(A, B) ->
    A `xor` B.

%% Helper for list replacement (since Erlang lists are immutable)
lists:replace(L, Index, Element) ->
    lists:insert_at(Index, Element, lists:delete_at(L, Index)).


solve(N, Edges) ->
    % 1. Initialize DSU structure
    InitialParent = lists:seq(0, N-1),
    InitialParity = lists:seq(0, N-1),
    InitialDsu = #dsu{parent => InitialParent, parity => InitialParity},
    
    % State: {CurrentDsu, EdgeCount}
    State = {InitialDsu, 0},
    
    % 2. Process edges sequentially
    {FinalDsu, FinalCount} = lists:foldl(process_edge, State, Edges),
    
    FinalCount.

process_edge({CurrentDsu, Count}, [U, V, W]) ->
    % Find roots and parities *before* potentially merging
    {RootU, ParityU} = find_root(CurrentDsu, U),
    {RootV, ParityV} = find_root(CurrentDsu, V),
    
    if RootU == RootV ->
        % Cycle check
        % Cycle parity = Parity(U->V existing) XOR W
        % Path U -> V existing parity = ParityU XOR ParityV
        CycleParity = ParityU `xor` ParityV `xor` W,
        
        if CycleParity == 0 ->
            % Even cycle, add the edge
            {CurrentDsu, Count + 1}
        else ->
            % Odd cycle, skip the edge
            {CurrentDsu, Count}
        end;
    else
        % No cycle, attempt union
        {NewDsu, Success} = dsu_union(CurrentDsu, U, V, W),
        
        if Success ->
            {NewDsu, Count + 1}
        else
            % Should not happen if roots are different, but handle defensively
            {CurrentDsu, Count}
        end
    end.
